Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Oren Noah of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Albert Thuesen of Jackson & Wallace (982.6300) for defendant and moving party

Argued. Plaintiff's request for a continuance was denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CHAMPION INDUSTRIAL CONTRACTORS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant has shifted the burden of proof based on plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses and the Champion Declaration. Plaintiff's objections the declaration are overruled. Plaintiff fails to present evidence that raises a triable issue of fact. The Cohen Declaration fails to provide a basis from which a reasonable trier of fact could conclude that defendant knew or should have known of the risks described. The Cohen declaration makes reference to substantial scientific knowledge on the issue but it is silent as to the level of knowledge in the construction industry as a whole or specifically mechanical contractors. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780

Argued. Plaintiff's request for a continuance was denied. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT CAMPBELL SOUP COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Oren Noah of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Lisa Rickenbacher of Brydon Hugo & Parker (808.0300) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied. The motion is based on decedent's lack of exposure to asbestos from defendant's boilers. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. The Tracy declaration does not establish that the boiler could not have reached the Contadina site through another source. Jim Whinnery's deposition establishes exposure and decedent's deposition establishes par-occupational exposure. Plaintiff also presents declarations of Jim Whinnery and Ken Cohen that establish a triable issue of fact. Defendant's objections to evidence are overruled. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780
